claim: All hematopoietic stem cells segregate their chromosomes randomly.

result.claim: All hematopoietic stem cells segregate their chromosomes randomly.
result.reasoning: The claim is strongly supported by evidence from paper ID 4381486, titled "Haematopoietic stem cells do not asymmetrically segregate chromosomes or retain BrdU". The abstract of this paper explicitly states: "Sequential administration of 5-chloro-2-deoxyuridine and 5-iodo-2-deoxyuridine indicated that all HSCs segregate their chromosomes randomly. Division of individual HSCs in culture revealed no asymmetric segregation of the label." This finding directly confirms that hematopoietic stem cells (HSCs) segregate their chromosomes randomly, refuting the "immortal strand hypothesis" in the context of HSCs. No contradicting evidence was found in the initial search or among similar papers.
